posture: Quiet end-of-week scan — no confirmable new, in-scope innovation signal surfaced in the last 24 hours. The sector's active frontiers (FOGO/circular economy, AI-assisted development assessment, smart communities) remain warm but produced no fresh, in-window council announcement today; the week's strongest 'adopt' material remains the 2026 National Awards for Local Government already logged on 29 June.
summary: Empty day. A full daily scan of the §1a–§1d free sources (AU council & sector press, AU industry verticals, overseas equal-weight outlets and the tertiary/research institutes) returned no item that was simultaneously (a) published in the last 24 hours, (b) in scope per the §3b Australian-council-function filter, and (c) not already covered in the previous 3 days. Several recurring or undated items were considered and excluded: the staged NSW FOGO mandate (large-business phase commenced 1 July 2026) and related kerbside-standardisation milestones are regulatory-rollout dates rather than fresh innovation signals; ongoing NSW AI-in-planning activity (the multi-council Early Adopter AI trial, DAISY development-application assistant) produced no new dated development today; recycled-content road/pavement material (ARRB recycled crushed-glass asphalt with Brimbank, crumb-rubber trials on Victorian roads, Recycled First) surfaced only via older or undated coverage; the City of Stirling Coolbinia FOGO trial early-results release traces to earlier reporting; the NSW EPA 'problematic waste' council grants (~$2.3M) were undated in-window; and the AU$15M National Digital Twin unveiled in Adelaide is an agriculture/space-agency flagship, out of scope under §3b (not a council function). No overseas item cleared the scope filter as a fresh, dated signal — UK pothole/road-defect AI coverage (Cambridgeshire, Hertfordshire) was recycled or out-of-window. Today is a Friday, so the §1e 50-council watchlist deep scan (Saturdays only) was not run. No paywalled items were logged; where a paid title was the only source it was held to headline + standfirst and not used. Sections are published empty per §6.
